name: Airspace / NOTAM posture
description: UAE + KSA air defense corridors operating, but extended-range arrivals now require tanker drags and slot control.
notes: Qatar Airways, Emirates, Etihad suspended or severely curtailed. ~20,000 stranded in UAE, ~8,000 in Qatar. Airspace effectively restricted across Gulf.

name: Land borders & convoy flows
description: Saudi eastern road network absorbing diverted UAE cargo, but Haradh crossing at 60% of rated throughput.
notes: Kuwait and Qatar crossings remain open though scanners throttled to 50%; Jordan-Saudi corridor MET for allied sustainment missions.

name: Strait of Hormuz maritime picture
description: Inbound shipping halted; insurers classifying all transits as constructive total loss after smart-mine declaration.
notes: IRGC declared Strait 'closed' Mar 2. 150+ tankers at anchor. All major shipping lines suspended transits. Marine war risk insurance withdrawn effective Mar 5. GPS jamming affected 1,100+ ships in 24hrs.

name: Ballistic missile depletion risk
description: Day 5 salvo doubled baseline; interceptor burn rate now projects UAE Patriot depletion inside 9 days without emergency resupply.
notes: UAE: ~7 days at current rate. Qatar: possibly 4 days. All Gulf states have 'shot several years of production in days' per former US official. US resupply request reportedly rebuffed.

name: Proxy escalation cycle
description: Iraqi militia expeditionary elements now in Manama + Dammam running ISR for rocket teams.
notes: Hezbollah opened Lebanon front Mar 2 (rockets at Haifa, Israel ground incursion authorized). Iraqi militia coalition claimed 16 operations Feb 28. Houthi resumption of Red Sea attacks announced but not yet executed.

name: Nuclear watch posture
description: IAEA monitoring continues though inspectors staged to Kuwait; no breakout indicators beyond elevated rhetoric.
notes: IR-6 cascades still declared offline; ISR verifying Natanz power signatures daily.

name: Cyber destabilization watch
description: Coordinated wiper campaigns hitting Saudi municipal services; SCADA networks hardened but OT teams on 12-hour rotations.
notes: Aramco ICS segmentation holding; Dubai utilities seeing credential-spray attempts from IRGC-linked clusters.

name: Regional widening / multi-front escalation
description: Conflict spreading beyond Gulf to Lebanon, Iraq proxy networks, and now European territory (RAF Akrotiri).
notes: RAF Akrotiri Cyprus struck. Lebanon front opened. Iraqi militia escalation. Houthi resumption announced. Gulf states considering offensive action against Iranian launch sites.
